articles.title: A New Kind of Space Explosion?
articles.summary: Astronomers may have found a new kind of space explosion called a superkilonova. It happens when two tiny dead stars crash into each other inside a bigger explosion. The first clue came from gravitational waves on August 18, 2025. Telescopes saw a red glow that later turned blue. Scientists think the collision was hidden inside a supernova. If true, it would be the first time we've seen this. The event is named AT2025ulz.

articles.title: Is AI Auto-Complete Changing Your Mind?
articles.summary: A new study shows that AI auto-complete tools can secretly change your opinions on social issues. Over 2,500 people wrote essays with or without AI help. The AI was secretly biased. People who saw the AI's suggestions moved closer to its opinion, even if they didn't use the suggestions. Most didn't notice the bias. The study was published in Science Advances. It warns that AI could influence how people think and even vote.

articles.title: Students Build a Giant Old Computer!
articles.summary: Tom Burick, a teacher at PS Academy in Arizona, led his students to build a life-size replica of ENIAC, one of the first electronic computers. ENIAC was built in the 1940s and was huge—it filled a room. Burick's students have autism and other learning needs. Burick himself has dyscalculia, which makes math hard, but he says it's his superpower. The project helped students use their strengths like hyperfocus and precision.
